# npm-exists

Check if an npm package name is taken. Zero dependencies, uses native fetch.

## Summary

`npm-exists` is a minimal Node.js library and CLI tool that queries the npm registry (or any npm-compatible registry) to check whether a package name is already registered. Uses a HEAD request (no body). Returns the npm page URL string on success, or `false` for a 404.

## Installation

npm install @jayf0x/npm-exists

## Core API

```js
import npmExists, { getNpmUrl } from '@jayf0x/npm-exists'

// Returns URL string or false
const url = await npmExists('react')
// → 'https://www.npmjs.com/package/react' | false

// Custom registry
const url = await npmExists('my-pkg', 'https://my.private.registry.io')

// Suppress errors
const url = await npmExists('react', { silent: true })

// Returns registry API URL string
const apiUrl = getNpmUrl('react') // https://registry.npmjs.org/react
```

## Return type

`Promise<string | false>` — always. String = npm page URL, false = not found.

## CLI

```
npm-exists <package-name> [registry-url]
```

Exit 0 = exists, Exit 1 = not found.

## Requirements

Node >= 18 (native fetch). Zero runtime dependencies.

## Source

https://github.com/jayf0x/npm-exists
